properties.remark: SACRAMENTO COUNTY OES CONFIRMED LEVEE ALONG THE COSUMNES RIVER BREACHED IN AT LEAST TWO LOCATIONS OVERNIGHT, CAUSING FLASH FLOODING IN THE SURROUNDING AREAS. HIGHWAY 99 BETWEEN DILLARD RD AND GRANTLINE RD FLOODED WITH NUMEROUS VEHICLES STUCK IN FLOOD WATERS, REQUIRING WATER RESCUES. HIGHWAY 99 WAS CLOSED AROUND MIDNIGHT DUE TO FLOODING.
*** 1 FATAL *** 1 FATALITY IN A SUBMERGED CAR CONFIRMED BY CONSUMES FIRE NEAR SR 99 AND DILLARD RD. A LEVEE BREACH ON THE CONSUMES RIVER CAUSED THE FLASH FLOOD.

*** 1 FATAL *** CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTING A NEW FATALITY ASSOCIATED WITH THE DEC 31 / JAN 1 FLASH FLOOD/LEVEE BREAK. FEMALE BODY WAS FOUND OUTSIDE A VEHICLE NEAR DILLARD ROAD EAST OF CA-99. THIS IS THE SECOND CONFIRMED FATALITY FROM THIS EVENT. LSR REPRESENTS THE TIME THE BODY WAS FOUND.

*** 1 FATAL *** SACRAMENTO COUNTY SHERIFF OFFICE CONFIRMED A THIRD FATALITY ASSOCIATED WITH THE DEC 31 / JAN 1 FLASH FLOOD / LEVEE BREAK. BODY WAS FOUND INSIDE A SUBMERGED VEHICLE AT NEW HOPE ROAD WEST OF ORR ROAD WEST OF GALT. LSR REPRESENTS THE TIME THE FATALITY WAS CONFIRMED AS ACTUAL TIME IS UNKNOWN DUE TO EXTENSIVE FLOODWATERS.
POWER POLE AND TREE LEANING OVER AGUAS FRIAS RD.
DAMAGE TO METAL ROOFING AND SIDING ON A HAY BARN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
PER CHP SEMI TRUCK OVERTURNED AND HANGING OFF THE SIDE OF THE INTERSTATE 5 BRIDGE OVER THE YOLO BYPASS.
A LARGE HORSE BARN WAS PICKED UP AND MOVED OVER/ACROSS A 5 FOOT FENCE FROM A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. BASED ON RADAR AND DAMAGE PHOTOS, WINDS WERE AROUND 75 MPH.

BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR SMALL RESERVOIR AND HOGAN DAM ROAD AND CONTINUED NE FOR APPROXIMATELY 0.4 MILES. IT CAUSED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE TO WHITE OAK AND PINE TREES WITH TREES UPROOTED AND TOPPED. APPROXIMATE PATH LENGTH OF 0.4 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS AND MAX WINDS OF 90 MPH.
EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE IN THE GOLF COURSE COMMUNITY OF GREENHORN CREEK.
TREE DOWN ACROSS ROADWAY.
PER CHP LARGE TREE DOWN BLOCKING BOTH LANES. ROAD CLOSED.
LARGE TREE LIMB BLOCKING WESTBOUND LANE.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING BOTH LANES.
2 TREES DOWN INTO LINES. ROADWAY ENTIRELY BLOCKED.
TREE DOWN ACROSS ROADWAY.
LARGE TREE DOWN BLOCKING BOTH LANES.
TREE DOWN ACROSS ROADWAY.
TREE IN ROADWAY.
LARGE TREE DOWN BLOCKING WHOLE ROADWAY.
TREE DOWN IN ROADWAY.
TREE AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS WHOLE ROADWAY.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING MOST OF BOTH LANES.
LARGE TREE DOWN BLOCKING BOTH LANES.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING ROADWAY. COMMS LINES DOWN.
TREE AND POWERLINES DOWN BLOCKING ROADWAY.
CHP REPORTS 3 POWERLINE POLES DOWN WITH LINES ON THE GROUND.
CHP REPORTS A TREE DOWN INTO POWER LINES ON LA PORTE RD.
CHP REPORTS A LARGE PINE TREE DOWN ACROSS BOTH LANES OF SR 99 JUST NORTH OF E 1ST AVE.

A BRIEF, WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO 3 RESIDENCES ALONG KIRKWOOD STREET TO THE EAST OF HERALD. ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO GARAGES WAS OBSERVED. AN RV SHELTER STRUCTURE AS WELL AS DOG KENNELS WERE LIFTED UP OFF OF THE GROUND AND SUSTAINED DAMAGE. THE PRELIMINARY DETERMINATION IS THAT THIS WAS AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH A WIDTH OF APPROXIMATELY 50 YARDS, A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 0.3 MILES, THAT TOUCHED DOWN FOR APPROXIMATELY 2 MINUTES WITH 80 MPH PEAK WIND.
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED ALONG HIGHWAY 45 JUST SOUTH OF COUNTY ROAD 26. REPORT VIA TWITTER.
1-3 INCHES OF HAIL ACCUMULATION OVER THE ROADWAY (HIGHWAY 45 JUST SOUTH OF COUNTY ROAD 26). REPORT VIA TWITTER.
CREWS WORKING ON SR-49 SOUTH OF DOWNIEVILLE IN SIERRA COUNTY JUST FINISHED CLEARING 30 YARDS OF ROCK, DIRT, SNOW & TREES IN A SLIDE TRIGGERED BY HEAVY OVERNIGHT RAIN.
REPORT OF HAIL WITH HAIL STONES UP TO QUARTER SIZE IN STOCKTON. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
PUBLIC REPORTED HAIL UP TO QUARTER SIZE ALONG HIGHWAY 26 NEAR BEYER LANE. PHOTOS CONFIRMED. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NUMEROUS HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. WOODEN POWER POLE SNAPPED IN HALF. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 0.8 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS LONG. EF 1 WITH 90 MPH WINDS.
EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE AT WOODS CREEK MOBILE HOME PARK. 34 TREES DOWN.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWER POLES DOWN NEAR INTERSECTION OF MARTIN LANE AND CAMPO SECO ROAD.
TUOLUMNE COUNTY OES REPORTS APPROXIMATELY 25-30 HOMES IMPACTED, 10 PUBLIC BUILDINGS AND 30 BUSINESSES IMPACTED FROM THUNDERSTORM FLASH FLOODING ON SATURDAY.
A STORM SPOTTER REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL DOWNTOWN SONORA. THEY ALSO SAID THE CREEK DOWNTOWN HAS REACHED ITS BANKS.
FLASH FLOODING ONGOING IN THE DOWNTOWN AREA OF SONORA DUE TO SEVERE THUNDERSTORM. CREEK FLOWING OUT OF BANKS. WATER FLOWING OVER WASHINGTON STREET.
SIGNIFICANT ROADWAY FLOODING WITH 2 HOUSES FLOODED.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED SIGNIFICANT ROADWAY FLOODING IN THE TOWN OF TUOLUMNE WITH CREEKS OVERFLOWING THEIR BANKS.
CALAVERAS COUNTY PUBLIC WORKS ROAD CLOSURE OF COOPER COVE DRIVE BETWEEN BLACK CREEK DRIVE AND CHEYENNE ROAD IN COPPEROPOLIS DUE TO SEVERE ROAD DAMAGE BY FLOODING.
MAJOR DAMAGE DUE TO A MUDSLIDE INTO A RESIDENCE ON BEN TAYLOR ROAD IN COLFAX (PLACER COUNTY) ALL OCCUPANTS ARE ACCOUNTED FOR. NON-INJURY. MANY ROADS CLOSED IN THE AREA.
A MUD SLIDE HAS BEEN REPORTED ON HIGHWAY 89 EAST OF PAXTON BLOCKING TRAFFIC.
STRONG STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS DOWNED MULTIPLE TREES AND UTILITY POLES NEAR DOWNTOWN WOODLAND. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MUD/DIRT/ROCK BLOCKING WB LANE.
HAILLE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SR 99N/6TH ON-RAMP NORTHBOUND MUDSLIDE ON THE ON RAMP. ON-RAMP CLOSED.
Mud/Dirt/Rock slide highway 70 at serpentine curves.

California Highway Patrol-Truckee reported the closure of portions of Donner Pass Road (Old Highway 40) due to a rock slide/large boulder in the road between Sugar Bowl and Truckee after thunderstorms brought locally heavy rain the day before.
CHP incident report of multiple slides mud/dirt/rock on SR 70 completely blocking roadway.
CHP incident report of mud/dirt/rock slides west of Hot Springs Ravine on SR 70/Feather River Scenic Bywy.
CHP incident report of multiple slides on SR 70 & Caribou Rd. Blocking roadway.

State Route 89 approximately 3 mi north of Greenville Wye was blocked due to a debris flow at 4:08 PM on June 12, 2023. The road was opened to one way traffic by 4:19 PM on June 12, 2023, and was fully cleared to through traffic by 7:01 AM on June 13, 2023.
State Route 70 was closed between the Greenville Wye and Jarbo Gap due to strong winds causing a boulder to fall onto the roadway and damaging the pavement.

Caltrans District 2 reported a slide along state route 70 just south of PG and E Rock Powerhouse due to rainfall over the last 24 to 48 hours. The slide was reportedly 3 feet in diameter. The road closure lasted approximately 2 hours and the road reopened at 850 am.
CHP/Cal Trans reported rock slide on road near Potato Patch Campground. Rocks were in both lanes and are being cleared by Cal Trans.
CHP is reporting Mud/Dirt/Rock starting to come down hill and cross roadway. US50/Bridal Veil Falls, east bound.

NWS Meteorologists surveyed damage in Oroville from Tuesday evenings storms. It was determined that straight-line winds caused the tree and structure damage on the north side of Oroville with estimated winds of 60 to 90 mph.

Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 2 NNE Oroville. NWS Meteorologists surveyed damage in Oroville from Tuesday evenings storms in conjunction with radar and video evidence. It was determined that an EF-1 tornado caused tree and minor structure damage on the north side of Oroville. Max estimated winds 90 mph, path length 0.4 miles, width approx 90 yards.
